workflow.name: Match brands with influencers using GPT‑4.1, Google Sheets and SendGrid
workflow.description:
This workflow automatically matches brands with high-converting influencers by analyzing audience insights, engagement patterns, niche alignment, and historical conversion metrics — then delivers a ranked shortlist with detailed match rationale.

### Who's it for
• Brand managers launching influencer campaigns
• Marketing agencies managing multiple brand clients
• Growth teams running performance-driven influencer programs

### How it works / What it does
1. Accepts brand requirements via webhook (industry, audience, budget, KPIs)
2. Pulls influencer data from CRM / database or a Google Sheet roster
3. AI analyzes niche alignment, audience overlap, and engagement quality
4. Python scoring engine ranks influencers by conversion probability
5. Filters are applied based on brand budget and minimum thresholds
6. AI generates match rationale and performance predictions
7. Delivers ranked report via email and logs results to Google Sheets

### How to set up
1. Import this workflow
2. Configure credentials (Gmail/SendGrid, Google Sheets, OpenAI/Anthropic)
3. Populate your influencer data source (Google Sheet or CRM webhook)
4. Activate workflow and POST brand requirements to the webhook URL

### Requirements
• Webhook endpoint (or manual trigger for testing)
• Google Sheets (influencer roster + results tracker)
• OpenAI / Anthropic / Grok API key
• SendGrid or Gmail for delivery

### How to customize
• Adjust scoring weights in the Python node
• Change AI tone/output format in the AI nodes
• Add more filter criteria in the Filter node
• Extend the Google Sheet schema with additional columns

Want a custom workflow designed for your business? Our specialists can build it for you in no time—[Get in touch with our team](https://www.oneclickitsolution.com/contact-us/)

parameters.content:
## AI Influencer Matchmaking Engine

This workflow automatically matches brands with high-converting influencers by analyzing audience insights, engagement patterns, niche alignment, and historical conversion metrics — then delivers a ranked shortlist with detailed match rationale.

### Who's it for
• Brand managers launching influencer campaigns
• Marketing agencies managing multiple brand clients
• Growth teams running performance-driven influencer programs

### How it works / What it does
1. Accepts brand requirements via webhook (industry, audience, budget, KPIs)
2. Pulls influencer data from CRM / database or a Google Sheet roster
3. AI analyzes niche alignment, audience overlap, and engagement quality
4. Python scoring engine ranks influencers by conversion probability
5. Filters are applied based on brand budget and minimum thresholds
6. AI generates match rationale and performance predictions
7. Delivers ranked report via email and logs results to Google Sheets

### How to set up
1. Import this workflow
2. Configure credentials (Gmail/SendGrid, Google Sheets, OpenAI/Anthropic)
3. Populate your influencer data source (Google Sheet or CRM webhook)
4. Activate workflow and POST brand requirements to the webhook URL

### Requirements
• Webhook endpoint (or manual trigger for testing)
• Google Sheets (influencer roster + results tracker)
• OpenAI / Anthropic / Grok API key
• SendGrid or Gmail for delivery

### How to customize
• Adjust scoring weights in the Python node
• Change AI tone/output format in the AI nodes
• Add more filter criteria in the Filter node
• Extend the Google Sheet schema with additional columns

parameters.pythonCode:
# Scoring engine: calculates match score for each influencer against brand requirements
import json

item = _input.item['json']

brand_industry = item.get('industry', '').lower()
brand_age = item.get('targetAgeRange', '18-34')
brand_gender = item.get('targetGender', 'all').lower()
brand_locations = [l.strip().lower() for l in item.get('targetLocations', 'us').split(',')]
brand_budget = float(item.get('budgetUSD', 5000))
brand_min_eng = float(item.get('minEngagementRate', 2.5))
brand_min_followers = int(item.get('minFollowers', 10000))
brand_goal = item.get('campaignGoal', 'Brand Awareness').lower()
brand_platforms = [p.strip().lower() for p in item.get('preferredPlatforms', 'instagram').split(',')]

try:
    influencers = json.loads(item.get('influencerProfiles', '[]'))
except Exception:
    influencers = []

scored = []
for inf in influencers:
    score = 0
    reasons = []

    # --- Hard filters ---
    eng_rate = float(inf.get('engagement_rate', 0))
    followers = int(inf.get('follower_count', 0))
    cost_per_post = float(inf.get('avg_cpe', 9999))

    if eng_rate < brand_min_eng:
        continue
    if followers < brand_min_followers:
        continue
    if cost_per_post > brand_budget:
        continue

    # --- Niche alignment (0-30 pts) ---
    inf_niche = inf.get('niche', '').lower()
    if brand_industry in inf_niche or inf_niche in brand_industry:
        score += 30
        reasons.append('Strong niche match')
    elif any(kw in inf_niche for kw in brand_industry.split()):
        score += 15
        reasons.append('Partial niche overlap')

    # --- Audience demographics (0-25 pts) ---
    inf_age = inf.get('primary_audience_age', '')
    if brand_age in inf_age or inf_age in brand_age:
        score += 15
        reasons.append('Audience age aligns')

    inf_gender = inf.get('primary_audience_gender', '').lower()
    if brand_gender == 'all' or brand_gender in inf_gender:
        score += 10
        reasons.append('Gender demographic match')

    # --- Location overlap (0-15 pts) ---
    inf_locations = [l.strip().lower() for l in inf.get('top_locations', '').split(',')]
    loc_overlap = len(set(brand_locations) & set(inf_locations))
    if loc_overlap > 0:
        score += min(15, loc_overlap * 5)
        reasons.append(f'{loc_overlap} location(s) overlap')

    # --- Engagement quality (0-20 pts) ---
    if eng_rate >= brand_min_eng * 2:
        score += 20
        reasons.append('Exceptional engagement rate')
    elif eng_rate >= brand_min_eng * 1.5:
        score += 12
        reasons.append('Strong engagement rate')
    else:
        score += 5
        reasons.append('Meets engagement threshold')

    # --- Platform fit (0-10 pts) ---
    inf_platforms = [p.strip().lower() for p in inf.get('platforms', '').split(',')]
    platform_match = len(set(brand_platforms) & set(inf_platforms))
    if platform_match > 0:
        score += min(10, platform_match * 5)
        reasons.append(f'{platform_match} platform(s) match')

    # --- Conversion history bonus (0-10 pts) ---
    conv_rate = float(inf.get('conversion_rate', 0))
    if conv_rate >= 5:
        score += 10
        reasons.append('High historical conversion rate')
    elif conv_rate >= 2:
        score += 5
        reasons.append('Moderate conversion history')

    # Estimated conversion probability (rough model)
    conv_prob = round(min(95, (score / 110) * 100 * (1 + conv_rate / 100)), 1)

    scored.append({
        **inf,
        'matchScore': score,
        'matchReasons': reasons,
        'conversionProbability': conv_prob
    })

# Sort by match score descending
scored.sort(key=lambda x: x['matchScore'], reverse=True)

return {
    'json': {
        **item,
        'scoredInfluencers': scored,
        'totalCandidates': len(scored)
    }
}

parameters.responseBody: ={{ JSON.stringify({ success: true, requestId: $json.requestId, totalMatched: $json.totalMatched, topMatch: $json.rankedShortlist[0]?.handle || null, message: 'Match report generated and delivered.' }) }}

user.name: Oneclick AI Squad
user.bio: The AI Squad Initiative is a pioneering effort to build, automate and scale AI-powered workflows using n8n.io. Our mission is to help individuals and businesses integrate AI agents seamlessly into their daily operations  from automating tasks and enhancing productivity to creating innovative, intelligent solutions. We design modular, reusable AI workflow templates that empower creators, developers and teams to supercharge their automation with minimal effort and maximum impact.

data.details: The Code node allows you to execute JavaScript in your workflow.

The Filter node can be used to filter items based on a condition. If the condition is met, the item will be passed on to the next node. If the condition is not met, the item will be omitted. Conditions can be combined together by AND(meet all conditions), or OR(meet at least one condition).
